frozen_components: Gated SAE (trained once, frozen throughout CL sequence)
architecture.summary: SAE-FD is a continual learning method that operates in three stages: (1) training a frozen Gated Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) to decompose dense MLP activations into sparse, overcomplete feature vectors on diverse text; (2) fine-tuning each task with LoRA while capturing anchor samples' SAE-encoded activations; (3) applying sparse-aware distillation during subsequent tasks by comparing current and anchor feature vectors through the frozen SAE, using cosine direction loss and active-feature magnitude loss, with adaptive regularization strength targeting a fixed loss contribution ratio.

biblio.title: SAE-FD: Sparse Autoencoder Feature Distillation for Continual Learning of Large Language Models

claims.statement: SAE-FD achieves 52.70% average accuracy with only 0.46% backward transfer on TRACE with LLaMA-2-7B-Chat
Compared to GORP, SAE-FD improves accuracy by +2.3% and reduces forgetting by 34% (0.46 vs 0.70 BWT)
On Vicuna-7B, SAE-FD achieves 53.50% AA with 0.69% BWT, surpassing O-LoRA (43.42% AA) by +10.1% AA
On Mistral-7B-Instruct, SAE-FD achieves 57.80% AA, surpassing TreeLoRA (54.77%) by +3.0% AA with 2.3× less forgetting (0.95 vs 3.77 BWT)
Adaptive λ mechanism improves BWT from 2.38% (fixed λ) to 0.46% (adaptive), a 5.2× improvement
SAE-FD outperforms raw MSE distillation on Vicuna by +2.19% AA (0.69 vs 0.96 BWT) and on Mistral by +1.86% BWT (1.47 vs 3.33)
Combined loss (cosine + magnitude) achieves 52.70% AA and 0.46% BWT, outperforming cosine-only (51.29% AA, 0.51% BWT) and magnitude-only (52.57% AA, 0.84% BWT)

SAE-FD requires a pre-trained SAE per base model, adding one-time training cost that limits applicability to architectures where SAEs are unavailable or perform poorly; method stores float16 MLP activations for anchor samples (0.8 GB per task), growing linearly with task count; TRACE evaluates on a single fixed task order; published baselines use different experimental settings (e.g., GORP uses 1 epoch/task vs. official multi-epoch schedule, SEEKR uses full fine-tuning vs. LoRA)
